pi_items.source: O*NET / SOC taxonomy mapping for Computer Systems Engineers/Architects
Task description explicitly states 'You are a platform engineer' and deals with DevOps/build orchestrator systems.

setup_task.sh injects 5 specific bugs into the Python codebase involving cycle detection (graph theory), reverse output (topological sort), pre-release comparing (semantic versioning), bounds inclusion, and missing transitive propagation (cache invalidation).

privileged_info_summary: The task environment and its 18-package dependency graph are synthetically generated directly via setup_task.sh. The 5 bugs correspond exactly to the claimed features: semantic versioning (comparing pre-releases), graph theory (cycle detection false positives on diamond DAGs), constraint bounds, topological sorting (reversed build order), and cache invalidation (missing transitive invalidation). The occupation metadata strictly maps to the O*NET standard for Computer Systems Engineers/Architects (15-1299.08) under the Computer and Mathematical major group.
